name: Brazilian Olive Stuffed Pot Roast
recipeInstructions:
1. Drain 1 cup of ripe olives, reserving the liquor.
2. Chop the remaining ripe olives finely and mix with parsley, Parmesan cheese, 2 teaspoons chili powder, salt, and garlic.
3. Make about 15 slits in the roast using a long, thin-bladed knife or larding needle. Stuff the olive mixture into the slits.
4. Brown the roast on all sides in an oiled skillet or Dutch oven. Remove and discard any accumulated fat from the pan.
5. Add chopped onion to the meat and brown lightly.
6. Mix the tomato sauce with 1/2 cup of the reserved olive liquor and the remaining teaspoon chili powder, then pour over the roast.
7. Heat to boiling, turn down the heat, and cover the pan. Simmer for about 2 hours or until the meat is tender, basting once or twice.
8. Pare and cut carrots into thick diagonal slices. Wash zucchini and cut into halves.
9. Add carrots to the meat 30 minutes before it is done. Cook for 15 minutes.
10. Add zucchini and remaining ripe olives and cook for an additional 15 minutes.
11. When the meat and vegetables are tender, remove to a heated serving dish. Skim any fat from the pan liquid.
12. Mix flour with 2 tablespoons cold water. Gradually add some of the hot liquid into the mixture, then combine it with the remaining liquid in the pan.
13. Cook, stirring until the sauce thickens and boils. Serve with sauce over the meat and vegetables.
